dialogue.utterance: Dispatch Unit 3 for reported stroke in [CITY].
Hi, I'm Alex. What brought you to call 911 today, ma'am?
I'm having trouble speaking. It started this morning.
Can you tell me your name? Where are we now?
My name is Sharon. We're in [CITY]...
What day is it today, Sharon?
Oh, I'm not exactly sure.
What happened today?
I was sitting here and... I had this sudden trouble speaking.
Eyes are open and looking at me, partner.
Pupils are equal, round, and reactive to light.
You're not speaking easily right now. Can you point with your left hand if you understand?
hospital...
Lift your left arm as high as you can. Okay, great. Now your right arm?
Right side is weaker. She can lift it, but it's slower and less complete.
Airway is patent and self-maintained. No signs of obstruction.

dialogue.micro_intent: check breathing - Rate and depth of respirations
dialogue.utterance: Breathing is regular, non-labored, and at 14 breaths per minute.
SpO2 is 100%. No signs of desaturation.

dialogue.micro_intent: check circulation - Pulse rate and quality
dialogue.utterance: Pulse is strong, regular, and measured at 86 BPM.
Blood pressure is 154 over 78.
Skin appears pale, but no cyanosis or mottling noted.
Sharon, can you point towards me with your left hand if you are oriented?
She points left twice-alert, oriented x4.
Do you feel any numbness or drooping with this trouble speaking?
Right-sided facial droop observed.
Does it feel like anything is moving down through your arm?
Right arm weakness reported.
Sharon, do you know of any medical history that could be important?
She told us she had a stroke in August but no lingering effects.
What medications do you currently take?
Lists: Amlodipine, Lasix, and other reported medications. No known drug allergies.

dialogue.micro_intent: S.A.M.P.L.E. - events leading up to the event
dialogue.utterance: Were you doing anything unusual in the hours before you called?

dialogue.micro_intent: S.A.M.P.L.E. - events leading up to the event
dialogue.utterance: I was just resting at home in the morning.

dialogue.micro_intent: S.A.M.P.L.E. - events leading up to the event
dialogue.utterance: Had you eaten or had anything to drink recently?

dialogue.micro_intent: S.A.M.P.L.E. - events leading up to the event
dialogue.utterance: Last oral intake was breakfast, within 3 hours.
On a scale from 0 to 10, what would you say your pain is right now?
Does the pain spread anywhere else in your body?
Describes focal numbness on right side, localized to speech.

dialogue.micro_intent: head and neck exam - Pupillary reflex
dialogue.utterance: We're going to use a penlight to shine a light in your eyes.

dialogue.micro_intent: head and neck exam - Pupillary reflex
dialogue.utterance: Okay.

dialogue.micro_intent: head and neck exam - Pupillary reflex
dialogue.utterance: Pupils are equal and reactive.

dialogue.micro_intent: head and neck exam - Right facial droop
dialogue.utterance: Right-sided facial droop is present.

dialogue.micro_intent: head and neck exam - Mental status
dialogue.utterance: What's your husband's name?

dialogue.micro_intent: head and neck exam - Mental status
dialogue.utterance: Sharon doesn't know her husband's name but understands me.

dialogue.micro_intent: head and neck exam - Mental status
dialogue.utterance: What is today's date, Sharon?

dialogue.micro_intent: head and neck exam - Mental status
dialogue.utterance: Says "April something," and "Three-something year."
I'm going to listen to your lungs. Can you take a deep breath for me?
Bilateral breath sounds clear-no wheeze or crackles.
Gentle palpation on your abdomen-any pain or tenderness?
She says no pain. Abdomen is soft and non-tender.

dialogue.micro_intent: extremities and back exam - Gross motor function
dialogue.utterance: Do you mind if we check movement and strength in your arms and legs?

dialogue.micro_intent: extremities and back exam - Gross motor function
dialogue.utterance: Right arm is weakened; ability to move the right leg is intact.
Pulse trending down to 75 BPM-still regular.
Respirations remain 14 per minute.
Blood pressure is 132 over 42-diastolic has dropped significantly.
Blood glucose is 595-elevated reading.
12-lead ECG done. Rhythm is sinus with right bundle branch block, no acute injury.
Partner, give 1 L of normal saline to stabilize the diastolic drop and manage glucose.
Fluid bolus begun. IV placed in the left forearm, 18-gauge.
Let's begin 4-lead ECG since she's now on IV.
Done. Sinus rhythm still present, stable so far.
Recheck EKG and glucose post-bolus.
12-lead done again-no change in RBBB, still normal sinus with slight delay, left anterior fascicular block seen.
Blood glucose remains at 595, SpO2 100% still.
Let's alert the hospital-Stroke, all findings are consistent with a repeat event.
[COMPANY_NAME] is alerted with full Stroke protocol.
Second recheck-75 BPM, same as before.
14 breaths per minute for the second time.
Blood pressure remains 132/42-low diastolic, stable.
Second glucose check-595 still.
Second EKG unchanged-same RBBB and stable rhythm.
SpO2 still 100%-oxygenation is maintained.
Pain level remains at 2-lingering right-side weakness and speech problem.

dialogue.micro_intent: redo S.A.M.P.L.E. - events leading up to the event
dialogue.utterance: Has this gotten better or worse at all?

dialogue.micro_intent: redo S.A.M.P.L.E. - events leading up to the event
dialogue.utterance: It worsens when trying to walk or move right side.

dialogue.micro_intent: redo S.A.M.P.L.E. - events leading up to the event
dialogue.utterance: Is this the first time since your August event?

dialogue.micro_intent: redo S.A.M.P.L.E. - events leading up to the event
dialogue.utterance: Yes-completely new issues since this morning.
Sharon, do you agree to go to [COMPANY_NAME] for care?
[COMPANY_NAME] is closest with CT for stroke. We're leaving now.
Let's prep her for movement-straps and semi-Fowler's.
Patient is lying still-keep the right side supported.
Secured. Five straps down-semi-Fowler position confirmed.
ER is on standby and aware of stroke alert.
Conscious and stable but with right-side deficit.
Patient has a history of CHF, hypertension, and diabetes. Stroke last August with no lasting effects.
Right arm and facial droop present-monitor for speech as well.
IV on left arm-Normal Saline flowing. Blood glucose elevated.
EKG showed RBBB-anterior fascicular block. Vital signs are recorded within bundle.
Alert and oriented four. Cannot sign due to right-hand weakness.
[UNIQUE_IDENTIFIER] provided. No insurance card at the time of call.
Onboard for transport. RN at hospital is aware. Verbal handoff done.
Are there any remaining steps or medications?
No changes. IV stable, no adverse effects, no new symptoms.
Transportation was smooth-staying close should patient show decline.
All equipment to be returned-patient fully under hospital care now.
Thank you. You're free to return post.
Her pain level is still at 2 based on the most recent check.
Clinical assessment supports CVA. Right facial droop present.
Left anterior fascicular block is present on second EKG reading.
